artifact_name_en: Jade Gangmao amulet / Sheng-shaped pei ornament
ocr_text: the use of jade shes became popular during the Spring and Autumn Period, added with... ornamental, gradually removed from practical use, commonly referred to as jue with... gangmao amulets or sheng-shaped pei ornaments, both worn as pendants, were in vo... latter also called sinan. 1. 剛卯是隨身佩帶，具有「辟邪」功能的飾物，因為器表面積很小，故用簡筆字刻記驅邪的咒語。 Gangmao is a small amulet worn as pendants to ward off evils, with spells engraved in simplified characters for lack of space. 2. 「勝」是漢代人認為西王母頭上的裝飾，可用五、金等製作。勝形佩的造形是疊壓二個「勝」面成，中央相連處有穿孔，可穿繩繫帶。「勝」的造形與古代占卜的「式盤」或原始的指南用的「司南」是否有關尚有待研究。 Sheng, made of either gold or jade, was a style of hair jewelry worn by the Goddess of West Heaven, according to the people of Han dynasty. The sheng-shaped pei pendant is of two stacking shengs, with a hole where they join to allow a strap to go through. Further research is needed to find out if the form of sheng is related to those of ancient divination trays or early compasses (sinan). 南北朝 玉帶扣 Jade Sheng Pendant Eastern Han dynasty
caption: 解說牌附小型玉勝佩與玉剛卯，下方可見圓形帶頭與玉勝佩展品
notes: 上方為玉兵展區延伸文字，編號標籤 1、2、3

ocr_text: 「玉兵」與「玉具劍」 在西元前2000年以後，銅、鐵陸續成為製作帶刃器的主要質材。在階級森嚴的社會裡，飾有龍紋、雲紋的玉質工具、武器，主要用以象徵統治者高貴的身份。約自春秋時代開始，在劍與劍鞘的特定部位，飾以美玉。到漢代時發展成固定的四種玉飾：首、鐔、璏、摽（々ㄠㄟ）。四種玉飾齊備者，稱為「玉具劍」。十分貴重，可作為皇帝送給匈奴單于的國禮。 Jade Weaponry and Jade-Ornamented Swords After 2000 B.C.E., bronze and iron in turn became the main materials for sharp-blade weapons, while arms and tools made of jade with dragon or cloud patterns were privileges reserved for ruling classes only, signifying the owners' noble status in rigid hierarchical societies. Around the Spring and Autumn Period, beautiful jade started to adorn gentlemen's swords and eventually developed into four decorative fixtures during Han dynasty: shou (pommel), tan (cross-guard), wei (scabbard slide), piao (shape). Swords complete with all four ornaments, called yujujian (jade-ornamented sword), were highly valued and fit for state gifts from the Han emperors to the chieftains of Xiongnu.
caption: 「玉兵」與「玉具劍」展區大段中英介紹文字牆

caption: 展櫃內一柄附玉劍飾的青銅劍及散置玉劍飾配件
notes: 名稱牌文字反光，僅部分可讀

ocr_text: 喪葬玉 「喪葬玉」一詞指各類專為亡者製作，以強化亡者「精氣」，助其達於「再生」或「不朽」境界的各類玉器，包括： 一、西周晚期開始流行蓋住亡者面孔的玉面飾。 二、漢代時除玉眼蓋外，更用短柱形塞，塞住鼻、耳等人體孔竅。 三、春秋時期開始流行在亡者衣服上縫綴玉片，稱為「鱗施」。 四、漢代時最高級的葬服是用金、銀、銅、絲等綴縷，綴連玉片組成全套「玉匣」，將亡者包裹密實，只有頭頂部位縫綴的玉璧中孔，可供靈魂通過。 五、戰國時從楚文化區開始，流行用碧玉製作各式玉璧及成對玉珩、龍形佩，用於隨葬，西漢時有了「玉匣」制度後，在本組內用絲帛綑綁多件玉璧於亡者前胸後背，玉珩、龍形佩仍放在棺內。 六、較為一般的葬俗，是在亡者口中、手心，填以各式「哈」、「握」。到漢代時，流行含玉蟬，握玉豬，祈求亡者「再生」，家族能「繁衍興旺」。 Funerary Jades Funerary jades were specifically made to help fortify the deceased's jingqi (vital force) in the journey reaching next life or eternity, including: 1. Jade face covering, popular born in the 9th century. 2. Jade eye covers as well as jade plugs to seal off nostrils and ears etc., used in Han dynasty. 3. Jade slices sewn to burial garb, called scales, popular during the 8th to 3rd centuries. 4. Burial garb of the topmost grade in Han dynasty was a jade casing sewn of jade squares with gold, silver, copper, or silk threads, tightly enclosing the deceased inside. The only opening is a hole in the center of the jade bi disc sewn at the head end of the casing, for the soul to pass through. 5. During the Warring States Period, a new trend started from the Zhu Culture in the south which used dark green jade to fashion burial ornaments such as bi discs in various styles, pairs of heng pendants or dragon-shaped pei pendants. Then in Han dynasty, along with the custom of jade casings, bi discs were also tied to the chest and back of the deceased while heng and pei ornaments were placed inside the casket. 6. A more common practice was to put a jade piece inside the mouth and in each of the hands, the former called han and the latter wo. Jade cicadas and jade pigs were used as mouthpieces and palm pieces, respectively, during Han dynasty, wishing for the deceased to be reborn and the family to prosper.
caption: 「喪葬玉」展區大段中英介紹文字牆，下方露出展品邊緣

Jade face covering, popular born in the 9th century. 2. Jade eye covers as well as jade plugs to seal off nostrils and ears etc., used in Han dynasty. 3. Jade slices sewn to burial garb, called scales, popular during the 8th to 3rd centuries. 4. Burial garb of the topmost grade in Han dynasty was a jade casing sewn of jade squares with gold, silver, copper, or silk threads. 5. During the Warring States Period, a new trend started from the Zhu Culture in the south which used dark green jade to fashion burial ornaments. 6. A more common practice was to put a jade piece inside the mouth and in each of the hands, the former called han and the latter wo.

gallery_guess: 玉器-宋遼金元（文化的競逐與融合）
ocr_text: 文化的競逐與融合 儒家要求帝王「崇儉」，宋代宮廷日常生活器皿，因而多避美玉為之。反觀遼、金君主、貴族對玉的喜愛及使用少此顧忌，遂正史中甚至出現遼人向宋臣誇耀，其宮廷用玉之華美豐富非南方可比的記載。 遼、金、元原以游牧、漁獵為生，建立王朝後則將此生活形態訂為儀典，並設計成輿服、器物的紋樣。如「春水」、「秋山」即為契丹、女真族依季節行獵的實況映照，具有強烈的民族特色。除此之外，其貴族階級崇信佛教，故其玉器紋樣亦常引佛經典故，如「摩羯」、「鹿母蓮花生子」等。 Cultural Rivalry and Fusion Confucianism honored thrifty and required such of emperors. The Song palace life accordingly avoided beautiful jades in daily use, whereas the rulers and aristocrats of Liao and Jin showed off their love for jades with no scruples. There are even mention of incidents in official histories that Liao flaunted to Song envoys that their sumptuous palace jades far surpassed those of the southern regime. The northern regimes Liao, Jin, and Yuan were...
caption: 「文化的競逐與融合」展區大段中英介紹文字牆

ocr_text: 追慕三代的仿古玉器 宋代理學深度結合了釋、道的教義與思想，然其本質仍是儒學，因此除關注自然的運作，更在意人世的秩序，故追求合理的禮儀規範，仰慕三代。宋儒從歸納古器物形制、判讀銘文、比對歷史制度，官方與私人並出版圖錄，以更正國家舊日祭典用器的謬誤，落實對儒家經典的理解與實踐。復古風尚遂被帶動，古穆風格成為宋代美學的重要特質之一，對此後的玉器發展，影響甚深。 Jades in Antiquarian Style Modeled on Ancient Times New Confucianism in Song incorporating the teachings of Buddhism and Taoism paid great attention to both the operations of nature and the order of the human world. Accordingly they sought rational ethics and etiquettes as guidance and admired exemplary models set by ancient dynasties. Song Confucian scholars categorized ancient vessels, elucidated inscriptions, compared historical institutions, and published private or government catalogues to rectify mistakes in contemporary protocols of national ritual systems and vessels, all for the purpose of fulfilling the theory as well as practice of the ideals as taught in the Confucian Classics.
caption: 「追慕三代的仿古玉器」展區大段中英介紹文字牆

ocr_text: 象徵法統的「封禪」玉冊 五千年前的新石器時代，築壇祭天地山川等自然神祇的宗教儀式已萌芽。東周齊魯之境盛行以五行為學理基礎的陰陽學派。秦始皇、漢武帝等霸主於一統天下後，均利用這些具陰陽學派色彩的儒生，倡言「自古受命帝王，易常不封禪」的議論，以舉行封禪大典強調其政權的正統性。 「封禪」即帝王在天下太平時，赴泰山昭告天地及祖先的重要祭典。史上共七位皇帝曾舉辦，除顯現君王的志得意滿，更耗費鉅量的人力、物力，乃歷史中極具爭議的話題，然典禮中的祝禱文卻未必見載於正史，更添其詭譎的色彩。本院藏有二套自泰山出土之唐、宋禪地玉冊，正可以補足、刊正史籍的闕如與錯植，是非常珍貴的第一手史料。 Jade Album of Slips symbolic of Orthodoxy Five thousand years ago far back in the Neolithic Age already emerged the religious ritual of building altars to offer sacrifices to animistic deities of heaven, land, mountains, and rivers. During Eastern Zhou dynasty, states such as Qi and Lu practiced the five-element system preached by the metaphysical school of Ying and Yang. After unification of the land, centralized regimes of Qin and Han further exploited the concept, having its followers advocate that all ancient sage-kings when bestowed with the mandate of heaven would perform the ritual ceremony of Fong Shan in order to stress the orthodoxy, or legitimacy of the rule.
caption: 「象徵法統的封禪玉冊」展區大段中英介紹文字牆

gallery_guess: 玉器-寫實風玉器（隋唐宋）
ocr_text: 崇尚自然的寫實風 考古所見隋、唐玉器，許多是以寫實人物、花鳥為紋樣，迥異昔日神秘玄想的氣息。此一轉變，可能是受到從中亞傳入中國之玉石器的影響。宋代理學大盛，格物的精神使得對自然的觀察更全面、描述更細微，象生玉器遂因一些令人驚喜的細節營造，而顯得加倍靈動可人。一種所謂「玉圖畫」者，則更進一步於人、物之外，加添背景，將環境縮影至尺寸之間。遼、金、元朝本為草原游牧民族，與自然的互動密切，因而特別喜好並擅長製作這類玉器，常援用於生活各個層面。 Realism in Admiration for Nature Archaeological findings show that patterns adorning jade articles in Sui and Tang dynasties focused on secular human figures, flowers, and birds, drastically different from the mystic aura of fanciful meditation prevalent from earlier periods. The agent of change might have been jade and stone articles imported from Central Asia. During Song dynasty, new Confucianism thrived; its realistic approaches to things, i.e. finding out the fact and essence of truths, prompted an even more comprehensive observation of nature and more detailed...
